threats.title: LabubaRAT Masquerades as NVIDIA Software to Control Windows Hosts
threats.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a previously undocumented Rust-based remote access trojan (RAT) codenamed LabubaRAT that masquerades as NVIDIA software to blend into target environments. threats.title: RabbitMQ Flaws Could Leak OAuth Secrets and Expose Cross-Tenant Queue Metadata
threats.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of two access control-related flaws impacting the RabbitMQ message broker service that could allow attackers to leak OAuth client secrets, expose enterprise messaging infrastructure to takeover risks, and bypass tenant boundaries. threats.title: 11 Old Microsoft-Signed Linux UEFI Shims Could Let Attackers Bypass Secure Boot
threats.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered 11 old, Microsoft-signed, Unified Extensible Firmware Interface (UEFI) applications that could be abused to bypass Secure Boot on most systems using the modern firmware standard. threats.title: Study of 85 Crypto Wallet Extensions Finds Address Leaks and Cross-Site Tracking Risks
threats.summary: Researchers at KU Leuven tested 85 of the most popular crypto wallets that run as browser extensions and found that the wallets themselves leak enough to link and track the people using them. threats.title: How Pentera Turns AI Security Workflows into Validation Engines
threats.summary: AI security agents are starting to influence real security decisions. They summarize findings, prioritize remediation, recommend next steps, and help teams move faster.
